content.full_text:
60202

Lobbyist Accepting Free Tickets to Fundraiser                    m- 99-208

     Jerry Augustine asked whether a lobbyist could accept

a free ticket to a fundraiser from his employer.  Al Herndon

informed him that the lobbyist could accept the ticket so

long as he had not arranged for its purchase.

     Under the Hupp opinion we thought an arrangement may

have existed if the lobbyist told his employers which fund-

raiser he should purchase tickets to.  If this were the case,

we asked Mr. Augustine to submit additional facts.

State of California LCDU/DT many HOT name
                                                                                    DOXU 4
                                                         independent expenditure
                                                                                    ¥¥D08

Memorandum

To     :  Opinion Request Meeting Participants                   Date  :  Feb. 9, 1977


From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
            Ken Finney

Subject:  Item for discussion.

           Lobbyists are prohibited from making contributions to
        candidates.  However, nothing in the Act prohibits a
        lobbyist from "independently" making an expenditure sup-
        porting a candidate, and pursuant to our definitions
        this would not be a contribution if it were not done at
        the behest of the candidate.  Do we want to address this
        issue in the legislative package?

        KF:jo      In the opinion request meeting of Feb 14, (19)7,
                   staff determined that Section 86202 prohibited
                   both contributions and independent expenditures.
                   We should adopt a reg. setting this principle
                   forth.

embedding.qa_text: Lobbyist Accepting Free Tickets to Fundraiser Jerry Augustine asked whether a lobbyist could accept a free ticket to a fundraiser from his employer. Al Herndon informed him that the lobbyist could accept the ticket so long as he had not arranged for its purchase. Under the Hupp opinion we thought an arrangement may have existed if the lobbyist told his employers which fundraiser he should purchase tickets to. If this were the case, we asked Mr. Augustine to submit additional facts. **Memorandum** **To:** Opinion Request Meeting Participants **Date:** Feb. 9, 1977 **From:** FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Ken Finney **Subject:** Item for discussion. Lobbyists are prohibited from making contributions to candidates. However, nothing in the Act prohibits a lobbyist from "independently" making an expenditure supporting a candidate, and pursuant to our definitions this would not be a contribution if it were not done at the behest of the candidate. Do we want to address this issue in the legislative package? KF:jo In the opinion request meeting of Feb (4, 1977), staff determined that Section 86202 prohibited both contributions and independent expenditures. We should adopt a reg setting this principle forth.
embedding.first_500_words: Lobbyist Accepting Free Tickets to Fundraiser Jerry Augustine asked whether a lobbyist could accept a free ticket to a fundraiser from his employer. Al Herndon informed him that the lobbyist could accept the ticket so long as he had not arranged for its purchase. Under the Hupp opinion we thought an arrangement may have existed if the lobbyist told his employers which fundraiser he should purchase tickets to. If this were the case, we asked Mr. Augustine to submit additional facts. **Memorandum** **To:** Opinion Request Meeting Participants **Date:** Feb. 9, 1977 **From:** FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Ken Finney **Subject:** Item for discussion. Lobbyists are prohibited from making contributions to candidates. However, nothing in the Act prohibits a lobbyist from "independently" making an expenditure supporting a candidate, and pursuant to our definitions this would not be a contribution if it were not done at the behest of the candidate. Do we want to address this issue in the legislative package? KF:jo In the opinion request meeting of Feb (4, 1977), staff determined that Section 86202 prohibited both contributions and independent expenditures. We should adopt a reg setting this principle forth.
